_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how still images, moving images and sound use intertextual references — connections to other texts — to enhance and layer meaning.

_source.w: We are learning how still images, moving images and sound use intertextual references — connections to other texts — to enhance and layer meaning.
y: Recognising intertextual references enriches our understanding of the texts we encounter.
This skill is especially important for interpreting contemporary media, which is rich with references.
Understanding intertextuality helps us create more sophisticated and resonant texts of our own.
eg: We identify how a film scene references a famous painting or historical photograph.
We discuss how a music video uses visual references to other cultural texts to create meaning.
We analyse how advertisements use intertextual references to connect their product with particular values.
We explore how parody and satire rely on the audience recognising the source text.
We examine how authors of picture books use visual intertextuality to layer meaning.
We create a multimodal text that deliberately uses intertextual references to enhance its message.
